subject: Add registry framework

Patch Set 10: Code-Review-1

> Patch Set 9:
> 
> Should we consider basing this on the connectivity API?

Agreed. Haibin when we register with multicloudK8s plugin we should be registering with the connectivity api.

I have some more comments but will make them on Monday.

Patch Set 10:

> Patch Set 10: Code-Review-1
> 
> > Patch Set 9:
> > 
> > Should we consider basing this on the connectivity API?
> 
> Agreed. Haibin when we register with multicloudK8s plugin we should be registering with the connectivity api.
> 
> I have some more comments but will make them on Monday.
I am not sure I understand your mean.
Maybe you want to change it like below:

Put (2) code under (1)	
(1) connection delete API 
   instRouter.HandleFunc("/connectivity-info/{connname}", connectionHandler.deleteHandler).Methods("DELETE")
(2) Add Registry path
   registryHandler := registryHandler{client: connectionClient}
   registryRouter := router.PathPrefix("/v1").Subrouter()
   registryRouter.HandleFunc("/{cloud-owner}/{cloud-region}/registry", 
                             registryHandler.createHandler).Methods("POST")
   registryRouter.HandleFunc("/{cloud-owner}/{cloud-region}/registry/{id}", 
                             registryHandler.getHandler).Methods("GET")
   registryRouter.HandleFunc("/{cloud-owner}/{cloud-region}/registry/{id}", 
   registryHandler.deleteHandler).Methods("DELETE")

Patch Set 10:

> Patch Set 10:
> 
> > Patch Set 10: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > > Patch Set 9:
> > > 
> > > Should we consider basing this on the connectivity API?
> > 
> > Agreed. Haibin when we register with multicloudK8s plugin we should be registering with the connectivity api.
> > 
> > I have some more comments but will make them on Monday.
> I am not sure I understand your mean.
> Maybe you want to change it like below:
> 
> Put (2) code under (1)	
> (1) connection delete API 
>    instRouter.HandleFunc("/connectivity-info/{connname}", connectionHandler.deleteHandler).Methods("DELETE")
> (2) Add Registry path
>    registryHandler := registryHandler{client: connectionClient}
>    registryRouter := router.PathPrefix("/v1").Subrouter()
>    registryRouter.HandleFunc("/{cloud-owner}/{cloud-region}/registry", 
>                              registryHandler.createHandler).Methods("POST")
>    registryRouter.HandleFunc("/{cloud-owner}/{cloud-region}/registry/{id}", 
>                              registryHandler.getHandler).Methods("GET")
>    registryRouter.HandleFunc("/{cloud-owner}/{cloud-region}/registry/{id}", 
>    registryHandler.deleteHandler).Methods("DELETE")

Registry handler needs to:
-	Handle POST/GET/DEL of calls to "/{cloud-owner}/{cloud-region}/registry
-	On receiving a POST
o	Get node feature labels from onap/multicloud-k8s/src/k8splugin/internal/app/node (code to be written but we can write stubs for you to call and fill in working code later)
o	Format node feature labels into flavors
o	Return feature labels to AAI 
(some code has been written for other case here needs to be genericized and used https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/multicloud/k8s/+/98896 - we wouldn’t be updating status but openstack flavors for registered cloud)
o	Register k8s cloud to k8s plugin using connection.go “connectivity API” code if not already registered – we might have said this is not needed late in conversations, I can’t remember, but it would be helpful
-	On receiving a DELETE – I’m not sure what this is meant to do, we can ask Bin Yang, but I think we just delete the AAI info that would have been inserted

Patch Set 10:

edit: Sorry, it does not need to handle GET.
